title: Evaluating Moving Contracts: What Does "Full-Service Packing" Actually Include in Ethiopia?
excerpt: Full-service packing Ethiopia: moving contract terms, what's included, materials, labor, fragile items, exclusions, evaluating quotes, Addis movers.
content:
Evaluating moving contracts: what does "full-service packing" actually include in Ethiopia?

"Full-service packing" on Ethiopian moving quotes ranges from crew wrapping every glass to crew handing you tape and wishing luck. Contract evaluation separates marketing from scope—what materials, which rooms, who packs fragile, and what happens when box count explodes mid-job.

This guide decodes full-service packing clauses so you sign informed, not optimistic.

Full-service packing: typical inclusions

  • Cartons, tape, paper, bubble for standard household goods.
  • Crew packing kitchen, bedroom, living areas per inventory.
  • Disassembly of basic furniture—beds, tables—reassemble at destination.
  • Labeling boxes by room.
  • Loading and unloading tied to same contract.

Common exclusions to verify

  • Owner-packed designation: Boxes you pack may void fragile claims.
  • Specialty items: Art, pianos, safes, IT—often separate line.
  • Materials cap: "Up to 40 boxes" then per-box surcharge.
  • Unpacking: Full-service pack ≠ full unpack—ask explicitly.
  • Cleaning and debris haul-away: Usually extra.

Materials quality matters

Thin cartons for books fail—ask carton grade or see sample. Wardrobe boxes for suits versus folded in linen—specify if career wardrobe central.

Labor hours and crew size

Full-service should state crew count and estimated hours—or fixed price with inventory. Open-ended hourly packing invites drift.

Fragile and high-value protocol

Vendor-packed fragile needs defined—dish packs, cell dividers, TV boxes. Declared value alignment for electronics and art.

Questions before you sign

  1. How many cartons included?
  2. Who packs kitchen fragile?
  3. Is unpacking any room included?
  4. What is owner-packed exclusion on insurance?
  5. Are wardrobes and mattress bags included?

Active Movers full-service standard

Request itemized packing annex to main contract—room list, materials, specialty exclusions explicit.

Conclusion

Full-service packing in Ethiopia means what the contract annex says—not the headline. Evaluate materials, rooms, fragile protocol, caps, and unpack scope before sign—and full-service becomes full clarity.
author: Active Movers Team
